commands.review_summary:
Adds a Slurm academic sandboxes planning page and links it from the docs index, feature index, and bring-your-own-infrastructure guidance.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a documentation/product-contract PR rather than a bug report with a failing current-main path.
commands.review_followup: - Manual review is the right lane because the remaining blocker is maintainer product/docs approval, not a narrow automatable defect.

target.title: [codex] docs: plan Slurm academic sandboxes
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Slurm academic sandbox planning page that defines the product offer, recommended integration path, adapter lifecycle, security boundaries, validation contract, and future built-in-provider criteria.
- Links the guidance from the main docs index, feature index, and bring-your-own infrastructure docs.
- Keeps the first step docs/product-contract only: use `provider: external` for site-local Slurm adapters before adding a built-in `provider: slurm`.

## Research notes

- SchedMD documents `sbatch` as submitting a script that may wait pending before resources are granted, so the docs recommend `warmup` to absorb scheduler queue time.
- SchedMD's REST guidance says `slurmrestd` is not designed to be directly internet-facing, so the docs keep Slurm control-plane access site-local.
- JupyterHub BatchSpawner and Open OnDemand patterns reinforce the same campus shape: site-owned templates submit scheduler jobs and expose interactive endpoints, while each site keeps resource/account policy local.

References:

- https://slurm.schedmd.com/sbatch.html
- https://slurm.schedmd.com/rest.html
- https://github.com/jupyterhub/batchspawner
- https://www.chpc.utah.edu/documentation/software/ondemand.php

Closes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/325

## Validation

- `scripts/check-docs.sh`
target.author: zozo123
